data.current_description:
Creates a Checkout for a Reader.

This process is asynchronous and the actual transaction may take some time to be started on the device.


There are some caveats when using this endpoint:
* The target device must be online, otherwise checkout won't be accepted
* After the checkout is accepted, the system has 60 seconds to start the payment on the target device. During this time, any other checkout for the same device will be rejected.


**Note**: If the target device is a Solo, it must be in version 3.3.24.3 or higher.
data.summary_suggestion: Initiate a payment checkout on a card reader device
data.description_suggestion: Creates and sends a checkout request to a specific card reader device to initiate a payment transaction. The operation is asynchronous - the actual payment process begins on the device after acceptance. Requires the target reader to be online and available. After acceptance, the system allows 60 seconds for payment initiation on the device; subsequent checkout requests for the same device will be rejected during this window. Returns a 201 status with checkout details. Note: Solo readers require firmware version 3.3.24.3 or higher.

Description lacks clarity on inputs (what checkout request contains), outputs (what is returned), and the asynchronous nature's implications for AI agents. data.current_description:
Terminate a Reader Checkout stops the current transaction on the target device.

This process is asynchronous and the actual termination may take some time to be performed on the device.


There are some caveats when using this endpoint:
* The target device must be online, otherwise terminate won't be accepted
* The action will succeed only if the device is waiting for cardholder action: e.g: waiting for card, waiting for PIN, etc.
* There is no confirmation of the termination.

If a transaction is successfully terminated and `return_url` was provided on Checkout, the transaction status will be sent as `failed` to the provided URL.


**Note**: If the target device is a Solo, it must be in version 3.3.28.0 or higher.
data.summary_suggestion: Cancel an in-progress payment transaction on a reader
data.description_suggestion: Cancels the current payment transaction on a specific card reader device. The operation is asynchronous and may take time to complete on the device. The termination will only succeed if the device is actively waiting for cardholder input (e.g., card insertion, PIN entry, signature). Requires the target reader to be online. Returns a 202 status indicating the termination request has been accepted. Side effects: If a return_url was configured on the original checkout, the transaction status will be sent as 'failed' to that URL. Note: Solo readers require firmware version 3.3.28.0 or higher. No explicit termination confirmation is provided.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Terminate a Reader Checkout' is vague about the action (cancellation of payment). Description lacks clarity on inputs (which checkout/transaction), outputs (what is returned), and critical side effects (return_url callback behavior). data.current_description:
Creates a Checkout for a Reader.

This process is asynchronous and the actual transaction may take some time to be started on the device.


There are some caveats when using this endpoint:
* The target device must be online, otherwise checkout won't be accepted
* After the checkout is accepted, the system has 60 seconds to start the payment on the target device. During this time, any other checkout for the same device will be rejected.


**Note**: If the target device is a Solo, it must be in version 3.3.24.3 or higher.
data.summary_suggestion: Initiate a payment checkout on a card reader device
data.description_suggestion: Creates and sends a checkout request to a specific card reader device to initiate a payment transaction. The operation is asynchronous - the actual payment process begins on the device after acceptance. Requires the target reader to be online and available. After acceptance, the system allows 60 seconds for payment initiation on the device; subsequent checkout requests for the same device will be rejected during this window. Returns a 201 status with checkout details. Note: Solo readers require firmware version 3.3.24.3 or higher.

data.current_description:
Terminate a Reader Checkout stops the current transaction on the target device.

This process is asynchronous and the actual termination may take some time to be performed on the device.


There are some caveats when using this endpoint:
* The target device must be online, otherwise terminate won't be accepted
* The action will succeed only if the device is waiting for cardholder action: e.g: waiting for card, waiting for PIN, etc.
* There is no confirmation of the termination.

If a transaction is successfully terminated and `return_url` was provided on Checkout, the transaction status will be sent as `failed` to the provided URL.


**Note**: If the target device is a Solo, it must be in version 3.3.28.0 or higher.
data.summary_suggestion: Cancel an in-progress payment transaction on a reader
data.description_suggestion: Cancels the current payment transaction on a specific card reader device. The operation is asynchronous and may take time to complete on the device. The termination will only succeed if the device is actively waiting for cardholder input (e.g., card insertion, PIN entry, signature). Requires the target reader to be online. Returns a 202 status indicating the termination request has been accepted. Side effects: If a return_url was configured on the original checkout, the transaction status will be sent as 'failed' to that URL. Note: Solo readers require firmware version 3.3.28.0 or higher. No explicit termination confirmation is provided.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Terminate a Reader Checkout' is vague about the action (cancellation of payment). Description lacks clarity on inputs (which checkout/transaction), outputs (what is returned), and critical side effects (return_url callback behavior).
